title: Naturalistic fMRI and MEG recordings during viewing of a reality TV show
description: This dataset comprises simultaneous naturalistic fMRI and MEG recordings from 60 participants (30 per modality) who watched a 25-minute excerpt from a Chinese reality TV show. The study captures neural responses during passive video viewing and subsequent spontaneous mental replay, employing both conventional 3.0T fMRI and novel 64-channel optically pumped magnetometer (OPM) MEG systems. The dataset includes anatomical MRI scans, preprocessed functional data, and behavioral measures of stimulus engagement, providing a unique resource for investigating naturalistic brain dynamics across complementary neuroimaging modalities.
methods_description: fMRI data were acquired on a 3.0T Siemens Prisma scanner using T1-weighted MP-RAGE anatomical scans (1×1×1 mm voxels, TR=2300ms) and T2-weighted echo-planar imaging functional scans (2.5×2.5×2.5 mm voxels, TR=2000ms, 63 slices). MEG data were recorded using a 64-channel optically pumped magnetometer system (Quanmag) with 1000 Hz sampling rate and radial sensor orientation. Participants viewed a 25-minute video stimulus followed by a comprehension quiz and 15-minute eyes-closed rest period. Preprocessing included DICOM-to-BIDS conversion, defacing, fMRIPrep pipeline with MNI152NLin2009cAsym normalization, motion correction, and confound regression. Six MEG participants were excluded from inter-subject correlation analysis due to anomalous power spectral density patterns.

funding_references.funder_name: Shanghai Key Laboratory of Brain-Machine Intelligence for Information Behavior
West China Hospital of Sichuan University Biomedical Research Ethics Committee
